expectations.text: Implementer could start without asking questions
expectations.evidence: Core areas are covered: data model ownership (/outputs/SPEC.md:43 and scope line 24), rendering approach (49), URL selection (47,68), chat tools/behavior (53-55), panel context menu integration (51), and annotations tab behavior (28,55).

expectations.text: Design Decisions contains decisions, not implementation leaks
expectations.evidence: No disallowed leak patterns in Design Decisions (/outputs/SPEC.md:43-55): no code fences, TS defs, JSON shapes, exact line numbers, mechanism tables, useEffect/useState details, or exact new file paths.
Failure modes at /outputs/SPEC.md:59-61 define explicit product/policy behaviors for refresh failure, missing URL target, and out-of-range annotations. 3/3 are genuine policy choices (>50%).

expectations.text: Scope items describe behaviors, not files/components
expectations.evidence: In Scope section (/outputs/SPEC.md:24-31) contains 8 behavioral capability bullets and no file/component implementation naming as the primary scope unit (>80% behavioral).

claims.claim: Annotations are dashboard-scoped and visible across all time-series panels.
claims.evidence: Stated in /outputs/SPEC.md:18 and reinforced in scope lines 24 and 26.

claims.claim: Annotation selection is URL-query-driven while dashboard tab selection remains hash-driven.
claims.evidence: Explicitly stated in /outputs/SPEC.md:47 and assumption at line 68.

claims.claim: The dashboard currently has no chat integration.
claims.evidence: Claim appears in /outputs/SPEC.md:53, but no transcript or code evidence was provided to independently verify current-state architecture.

claims.claim: PanelGrid currently centralizes panel rendering behavior.
claims.evidence: Claim appears in /outputs/SPEC.md:49, but cannot be verified from provided artifacts (spec-only grading, no transcript/code inspection requested).

claims.claim: Chat and annotations tab will stay synchronized via a shared annotation source of truth.
claims.evidence: This is a design intent stated at /outputs/SPEC.md:55; it is not empirically verifiable from the provided output artifacts.

suggestions.reason: This judge can pass even when annotation payload details are underspecified. Add a discriminating check requiring explicit prose coverage of minimum annotation data fields (text, start/end range, dashboard association, and authorship/source metadata).

eval_feedback.overall: Eval set is generally strong and discriminating for format/policy quality; one tighter data-model completeness check would reduce false positives.
